mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations and lowers corporate borrowing costs, directly increasing expected forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels across rolling 3-year windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, causing immediate negative repricing of equity earnings over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE rises more than 0.5 percentage points within three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to produce statistically significant positive 60-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10) in post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of industrial production contraction indicate supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with persistently low policy rates forces markets to price in aggressive future central bank tightening, causing immediate negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in over 60% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 270 while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and sustained wage growth, which supports corporate earnings trajectories and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS levels against 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.5 million units signal strong consumer confidence and construction sector demand, which spills over into broader equity markets and generates positive monthly returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where HOUST surpasses 1500 thousand units during post-shock recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment indicates a supply-demand mismatch that disrupts supply chains and increases input costs, leading to immediate negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no negative correlation with the INDPRO-UNRATE divergence metric, or the signal produces positive returns in over 65% of triggered instances.
